25 April 2026 Military News

News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

  • SOUTHCOM's JTF Southern Spear continues to choke off illicit financing with string of successful maritime interdictions SOUTHCOM 25 Apr 2026 -- Six months since its inception at the direction of U.S. Southern Command, Joint Task Force Southern Spear has significantly disrupted illicit maritime activity within the Western Hemisphere, dealing a critical blow to narco-terrorist networks and other illicit or sanctioned activities.

    • Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • Cost of US Operation Against Iran Exceeds $61Bln - Reports Sputnik 25 Apr 2026 -- The cost of the US operation against Iran has exceeded $61 billion, according to the Iran War Cost Tracker.

  • Iran's strikes on US bases more destructive than publicly admitted: Report Press TV 25 Apr 2026 -- Attacks carried out by Iran on US bases in the West Asia region in response to US-Israeli aggression on the country caused more extensive damage than it has been publicly declared by US authorities, a new report shows.
  • Iran's military warns of decisive response if illegal US naval blockade continues Press TV 25 Apr 2026 -- Iran's central military command warns that the continued naval "piracy, blockade, and banditry" by American forces in the region will be met with a decisive response from the Iranian armed forces.
  • Iran yet to deploy 'significant' portion of its missile capability: Defense Ministry Press TV 25 Apr 2026 -- The Defense Ministry says a significant portion of Iran's missile capabilities remains unused, while highlighting the domestic production of over 1,000 types of weapons throughout the country.

  • Trump Cancels Witkoff and Kushner's Trip to Pakistan for Talks With Iran Sputnik 25 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said on Saturday he had canceled the trip of special presidential envoy Steve Witkoff and his son-in-law Jared Kushner to Pakistan for talks with Iran.
  • U.S. won't renew Iranian, Russian oil waivers: Treasury Secretary People's Daily 25 Apr 2026 -- U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Friday that exemptions allowing for the purchase of Russian oil already at sea would not be renewed after the current license expires on May 16.
  • Israel dragged US into war against Iran, State Department confirms Press TV 25 Apr 2026 -- The US Department of State has admitted that Washington entered a war of aggression against Iran on behalf of the Israeli regime.
  • Trump's envoy, son-in-law heading to Pakistan for talks with Iran: White House People's Daily 25 Apr 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump's envoy Steve Witkoff and son-in-law Jared Kushner are heading to Pakistan for talks with Iranian Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi this weekend, the White House said Friday.
  • Iran, US Delegations Head To Islamabad As Mixed Signals Cloud Prospects For Talks RFE/RL 25 Apr 2026 -- Iran's foreign minister has arrived in Islamabad as the United States prepares to dispatch senior envoys to Pakistan for what Washington described as a new round of negotiations to end the Iran war, even as Tehran publicly denied any meeting with American officials was planned.

  • Iran's foreign minister leaves Pakistan as Trump cancels visit by US envoys Press TV 25 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi left Islamabad on Saturday after holding discussions with senior Pakistani officials on regional security and a ceasefire with the United States, during which he presented Iran's demands on ending the eight-week war.
  • No Iran-US meeting planned in Islamabad: Iranian official Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 25 Apr 2026 -- An Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman said Saturday that top diplomat Abbas Araghchi arrived in the Pakistani capital, and no direct engagement with the US is planned, Anadolu Agency reported.
  • Iran Rules Out US Meeting in Islamabad, Rejects Media Reports Sputnik 25 Apr 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei said Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has arrived in Pakistan for talks with senior officials, as part of Islamabad's mediation efforts to end an American-imposed war of aggression and restore peace in the region.
  • Iranian FM to convey Iran's considerations regarding war's end in trip to Pakistan -- media People's Daily 25 Apr 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi will convey Iran's considerations regarding the end of the war with the United States and Israel in his visit to Pakistan's capital Islamabad, the semi-official Tasnim news agency reported Friday.
  • Iran not to enter talks under pressure, threats, blockade: Pres. Pezeshkian Press TV 25 Apr 2026 -- President Masoud Pezeshkian highlights the contradiction between the US's applying pressure tactics against Iran and its self-described will to engage in diplomatic process with the Islamic Republic.
  • April 25: Iran presents 'workable framework' to end war as US sinks deeper into internal turmoil Press TV 25 Apr 2026 -- On Day 57 of the imposed war, Iran demonstrated confidence and cohesion as the US strategy showed mutating signs of failure, contradiction, and internal turmoil.
  • Iran will deliver 'strategic deterrence' response if attacked again: IRGC Press TV 25 Apr 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has warned that Iran would increase the severity of its retaliation against any new attacks on the country by the United States or the Israeli regime.
  • US must learn historic lesson from crushing defeats in Tabas and Isfahan: Pezeshkian Press TV 25 Apr 2026 -- Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian has warned the world's arrogant powers to learn from history, as divine intervention delivered yet another crushing defeat to the United States in Isfahan—much like its humiliating failure in Tabas 46 years ago.
  • US blockade of Iranian ports 'collective punishment' of civilians: Top rights body Press TV 25 Apr 2026 -- Iran's High Council for Human Rights has condemned the United States' blockade of Iranian ports and the seizure of an Iranian commercial vessel, the Touska, as crimes surpassing maritime piracy and state terrorism, and amounting to collective punishment against millions of Iranian civilians.
  • Araghchi meets Pakistan's premier as he conveys Iran's views amid stalled talks with US Press TV 25 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has met with Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif in the Pakistani capital to discuss regional developments, amid efforts by Islamabad to revive stalled talks between Tehran and Washington to end the war.
  • Iran's judiciary chief warns US defeats in Isfahan, Tabas will be repeated in Persian Gulf Press TV 25 Apr 2026 -- Iran's judiciary chief has warned that Washington lacks the capability to impose a naval siege on the Islamic Republic, vowing that the historic defeats of American forces at Tabas and Isfahan will be repeated in the waters of the Persian Gulf.

  • Macron Continues To Push Goal Of Reopening Strait Of Hormuz RFE/RL 25 Apr 2026 -- French President Emmanuel Macron has reiterated that his focus is on efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Flintlock's 91st Civil Affairs Battalion Invests in the future of Côte d'Ivoire AFRICOM 25 Apr 2026 -- During Exercise Flintlock 26, U.S. Soldiers from the 91st Civil Affairs Battalion and Ivorian civil-military cooperation soldiers visited a primary school in Ouffoué-Diékro, Côte d'Ivoire, to foster community trust and support youth education. The ceremony honored the donation of school supplies to over 600 children, underscoring the commitment to the region's long-term development beyond traditional military operations. Following this successful outreach, the soldiers continued their engagement efforts at the Lycée Scientifique de Yamoussoukro, highlighting the spirit of Flintlock 26.

  • Yevhen Perebyinis held a meeting with Minister of Defence of the Republic of Estonia Hanno Pevkur Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine 25 Apr 2026
  • President Ilham Aliyev: The military-industrial complex is developing in both Azerbaijan and Ukraine Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 25 Apr 2026 -- "An exchange of views was held regarding military-technical cooperation, and there are very great prospects here as well," President Ilham Aliyev said during a joint press statement with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy.
  • Ukraine and Estonia align priorities for a mutually beneficial defence partnership Ukraine MOD 25 Apr 2026 -- The Minister of Defence of Ukraine, Mykhailo Fedorov, met in Kyiv with the Minister of Defence of Estonia, Hanno Pevkur. The parties agreed on key priorities for mutually beneficial cooperation, including joint weapons production and the development of defence capabilities.
  • Estonia Signs Defence Industry Development Agreement with Ukraine Estonia MFA 25 Apr 2026 -- In Kyiv, Minister of Defence of Estonia Hanno Pevkur signed a Letter of Intent with Ukrainian Minister of Defence Mykhailo Fedorov aimed at creating a framework for defence industry cooperation, including the promotion of joint production. The "Build with Ukraine" initiative combines Estonian technological solutions with Ukrainian combat experience, enabling the faster production of innovative defence industry solutions.

  • Chinese, Vietnamese coast guards conduct first joint patrol in Beibu Gulf in 2026: official release Global Times 25 Apr 2026 -- To further deepen maritime cooperation between the Chinese and Vietnamese coast guards and enhance law enforcement collaboration, jointly safeguard safety and stability in Beibu Gulf maritime area and fishery production order, the China Coast Guard 4303 flotilla and the Vietnam Coast Guard 8004 flotilla conducted the first joint patrol in the Beibu Gulf for 2026 from April 22 to 24. This marks the 30th joint patrol conducted by the maritime law enforcement department of China and Vietnam since 2006, according to an official release by the China Coast Guard on Saturday.

  • Computer-aided part of Han Kuang drills end, referenced overseas conflicts: Koo CNA 25 Apr 2026 -- The computer-aided segment of the annual Han Kuang military exercises concluded on Friday, following 14 days of intensive wargaming incorporating elements in recent international conflicts and other realistic scenarios to strengthen the decision-making and responses of Taiwan's armed forces.

  • Behnam Ben Taleblu: Russia-Iran Partnership Is 'Reshaping Modern Warfare' RFE/RL 25 Apr 2026 -- Iran's war effort extends beyond the Middle East. By supplying Russia with drones and enabling its long-range strikes in Ukraine, Tehran has already become an active participant in a major war on European soil -- a development that analyst Behnam Ben Taleblu, a senior fellow at the Foundation for Defense of Democracies, says represents a critical turning point in both Iran's confrontation with the West and the broader alignment of authoritarian powers.

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

    Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • Netanyahu Orders Major Strike Against Hezbollah Targets in Lebanon Sputnik 25 Apr 2026 -- Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu ordered the Israel Defense Forces (IDF) to launch a major strike against Hezbollah targets in Lebanon, the prime minister's office said on Saturday.
  • Four killed in latest Israeli strikes on south Lebanon despite ceasefire Press TV 25 Apr 2026 -- At least four Lebanese citizens have been martyred in two separate Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon, only two days after the Beirut government and the Tel Aviv regime extended their ceasefire for three weeks after a high-level meeting at the White House.
  • Ceasefire in Name Only: IDF Strikes Lebanon After 200+ Violations Sputnik 25 Apr 2026 -- The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) said it had struck armed rocket launchers belonging to the Hezbollah movement in three areas of southern Lebanon.

  • No regional, international power can force Hezbollah to disarm: Top official Press TV 25 Apr 2026 -- A high-ranking Hezbollah official says no regional and international power can force the Lebanese resistance movement to lay down arms as it is determined to continue the struggle against the Israeli occupation and aggression.

  • Hamas, resistance groups decry Israeli assaults on Gaza as 'full-fledged war crimes' Press TV 25 Apr 2026 -- Hamas and other Palestinian resistance groups have categorically denounced the latest wave of Israeli strikes on the Gaza Strip as "a bloody Zionist escalation and full-fledged war crimes."

  • The OIC Condemns the Israeli Occupation's Approval to Establish a Jewish School in the Sheikh Jarrah Neighbourhood of Occupied Al-Quds Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 25 Apr 2026 -- The General Secretariat of the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) strongly condemned the decision of the Israeli occupation authorities to approve the establishment of a Jewish school in the Sheikh Jarrah neighbourhood in the occupied city of Al-Quds (Jerusalem), which comes within the framework of the Judaisation policy aimed at changing the demographic, geographical, cultural and historical character of the city, obliterating its Arab identity, and igniting a religious conflict whose dangerous consequences cannot be predicted for the region and the world

    • Other Conflicts

  • Statement attributable to the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General - on Mali UN Secretary-General 25 Apr 2026
  • No: 76, 25 April 2026, Regarding the Terrorist Attacks in Mali Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 25 Apr 2026
  • Press release on terrorist acts in Mali Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 25 Apr 2026
  • OIC Secretary-General Expresses His Solidarity with the Republic of Mali Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 25 Apr 2026 -- The Secretary-General of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) is following with great concern the military operations that have targeted Bamako, the capital of the Republic of Mali, and certain regions of the country

  • UN mission chief urges conditions for credible ceasefire monitoring in eastern DR Congo People's Daily 25 Apr 2026 -- James Swan, head of the United Nations (UN) peacekeeping mission in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), also known as MONUSCO, on Friday called for effective ceasefire monitoring.

  • Saudi Arabia Condemns Drone Strikes on Kuwait's Northern Border Posts Launched from Iraq SPA 25 Apr 2026 -- The Ministry of Foreign Affairs expressed the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's condemnation and denunciation in the strongest terms of the targeting of two sites at the northern land border posts of the brotherly State of Kuwait by drones launched from the Republic of Iraq

News Reports

  • China warns against U.S. export control bills, vows to defend firms' interests People's Daily 25 Apr 2026 -- China on Saturday warned against a package of export control legislation of the U.S. House of Representatives, reiterating its opposition to the over-extension of the concept of national security and the abuse of export controls.

  • Foreign minister arrives in Eswatini after Lai cancels trip CNA 25 Apr 2026 -- Foreign Minister Lin Chia-lung (林佳龍) arrived in Eswatini on Saturday morning, days after Taiwan's President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) was forced to cancel his planned trip to southern Africa due to reported interference from China.
  • Taiwan rejects claim that Mauritius never approved Lai's flight permit CNA 25 Apr 2026 -- The Presidential Office on Saturday rejected a Mauritian media report saying that the African country had never granted overflight permission for President Lai Ching-te's (賴清德) now-canceled Eswatini trip, calling the claim "rumor-mongering."
  • Ministry of National Defense Issues Press Release Regarding "PLA Joint Combat Readiness Patrol" ROC MND 25 Apr 2026
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 25 Apr 2026

  • Iran arrests 'veteran' Mossad spy, separatist terrorists in nationwide operations Press TV 25 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Ministry of Intelligence has announced the arrest of a "veteran" spy working for the Israeli regime, along with 15 members of separatist terrorist groups, following operations in different provinces.

  • Myanmar's President Min Aung Hlaing Meets with Wang Yi PRC MofA 25 Apr 2026 -- On April 25, 2026 local time, Myanmar's new President Min Aung Hlaing met with Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Foreign Minister Wang Yi in Nay Pyi Taw.

  • The OIC Strongly Condemns the Attack on the State of Kuwait Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 25 Apr 2026 -- The General Secretariat of the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) has condemned in the strongest terms the attack on two northern land border posts of the State of Kuwait by two booby-trapped drones coming from the Republic of Iraq, in violation of the sovereignty and security of the State of Kuwait

  • Pakistan Launches Indigenous EO-3 Satellite, Marking Major Space Milestone Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 25 Apr 2026 -- The Pakistan Space and Upper Atmosphere Research Commission (SUPARCO) has successfully launched its indigenous electro-optical satellite (EO-3) from the Taiyuan Satellite Launch Centre, marking another significant milestone in the nation's space capabilities.

  • Egypt Becomes Security Oasis in Middle East - President Sputnik 25 Apr 2026 -- Egypt has managed to become a security oasis in the Middle East despite serious challenges, Egyptian President Abdel Fattah El-Sisi said on Saturday.

  • UK-UAE Joint Statement: April 2026 UK FCDO 25 Apr 2026 -- Joint Statement between UK Foreign Secretary The Rt Hon Yvette Cooper MP and UAE Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister H.H Sheikh Abdullah bin Zayed Al Nahyan

  • Statement by the High Representative and the Commission ahead of the 40 years since the Chornobyl disaster European External Action Service (EEAS) 25 Apr 2026 -- Forty years ago, the disaster at the Chornobyl Power Plant marked one of the gravest nuclear disasters in human history. Its true toll, long obscured by Soviet secrecy, has only become clearer over time. To this day, its legacy remains a stark reminder that ensuring nuclear safety depends on transparency, robust safeguards and international cooperation.

  • EU Warns Bosnia US Gas Project Could Threaten 1 Billion Euros In Aid RFE/RL 25 Apr 2026 -- The EU has warned Bosnia that designating a private US company as the main investor in a planned gas pipeline, aimed at ending four decades of dependence on Russian gas imports, could undermine the its EU accession and put access to aid from Brussels at risk.
